title: chamaecrista glandulosa var. andreana
intro: Chamaecrista glandulosa var. andreana, also known as the "Andreana sensitive plant," is a species of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The medicinal properties of Chamaecrista glandulosa var. andreana include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system, and it can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion for use in herbal medicine.
